sections.markdown:
# Network Security Overview — Cross-Vendor Analysis

This notebook provides a unified view across all 3 firewall vendors:
- **Palo Alto PAN-OS** (`log.source == "palo-alto-firewall"`, attributes: `paloalto.*`)
- **Cisco ASA** (`log.source == "cisco-asa"`, attributes: `cisco.*`)
- **Fortinet FortiGate** (`log.source == "fortinet-fortigate"`, attributes: `fortinet.*`)

All queries use DQL normalization to provide a consistent cross-vendor view.

---

## 1. Event Volume by Vendor
Total log count per firewall vendor in the selected timeframe.

## 2. Block Rate by Vendor
Percentage of blocked/denied events per vendor. Normalizes across different action value names.

## 3. All Events Over Time by Vendor
Stacked area chart showing event volume per vendor over time.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter in(log.source, "palo-alto-firewall", "cisco-asa", "fortinet-fortigate")
| fieldsAdd vendor = if(log.source == "palo-alto-firewall", "Palo Alto",
                     else: if(log.source == "cisco-asa", "Cisco ASA",
                     else: "Fortinet"))
| makeTimeseries palo_alto = countIf(vendor == "Palo Alto"),
                 cisco_asa = countIf(vendor == "Cisco ASA"),
                 fortinet = countIf(vendor == "Fortinet"),
                 interval: 5m

sections.markdown:
## 4. Blocked Events Over Time by Vendor
Line chart showing only blocked/denied events per vendor.

## 5. Action Breakdown Across All Vendors
Raw action values by vendor — shows how each firewall labels permit/deny actions differently.

## 6. Top 20 Blocked Source IPs (All Vendors)
Most frequently blocked source IPs across all 3 firewalls.

## 7. Top 20 Targeted Destinations (All Vendors)
Most frequently targeted destination IPs across all firewalls.

## 8. High-Risk Port Blocks Across Vendors
Blocked connections targeting high-risk ports (RDP, SQL, FTP, SSH, suspicious).

---

## Normalization Reference

| Field | Palo Alto | Cisco ASA | Fortinet |
|-------|-----------|-----------|----------|
| **Vendor** | `log.source = palo-alto-firewall` | `log.source = cisco-asa` | `log.source = fortinet-fortigate` |
| **Action** | `paloalto.action` (allow/deny/drop/reset-both) | `cisco.action` (permit/deny/built/teardown/auth-failed) | `fortinet.action` (accept/deny/dropped/server-rst/client-rst) |
| **Source IP** | `paloalto.src` | `cisco.src_ip` | `fortinet.srcip` |
| **Dest IP** | `paloalto.dst` | `cisco.dst_ip` | `fortinet.dstip` |
| **Dest Port** | `paloalto.dport` | `cisco.dst_port` | `fortinet.dstport` |
| **Rule/Policy** | `paloalto.rule` | `cisco.acl_name` / `cisco.message_id` | `fortinet.policyname` |
| **Blocked = NOT in** | `allow` | `permit, built, teardown` | `accept, ssl-login-succeed, login, logout, config-change` |
